response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with genuine developer-tooling interest — including direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI components in C/C++) and authoring test frameworks and libraries. However, the role's core requirements are largely unmet: there is no substantive Java/Kotlin/JVM depth, no Spring or enterprise-framework experience, and the recent career trajectory centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang rather than the JVM ecosystem. The tooling passion warrants a conversation only if the pipeline is thin.
strengths: Hands-on IDE internals experience building C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI
Strong developer-tooling track record (test runner, assertion library, MCP code generators)
Software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, community teaching and workshops
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin experience and only peripheral Java usage, versus required deep JVM expertise
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise Java framework background
Recent work (TypeScript/Node, DLang, GIS platform) is far from the enterprise JVM tooling domain
